subject: computer-science
description: Operating System MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: What is an Operating System (OS)?
options: A set of programs that controls, supervises and supports a computer's hardware and application packages
mcqs.explanation: An Operating System is a set of programs that controls, supervises and supports a computer system's hardware and application packages.

mcqs.q: What is the correct OS layer order from top to bottom?
mcqs.explanation: The standard OS layer structure from top to bottom is: User → Application → Operating System → Hardware.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT an operating system?
mcqs.explanation: Oracle is NOT an operating system — it is a relational database management system (RDBMS).

mcqs.q: Which of the following is an open-source operating system?
mcqs.explanation: Linux is an open-source operating system, freely available with its source code.

mcqs.q: Unix-based systems offer:
mcqs.explanation: Unix-based systems offer both a command line interface (CLI) and a graphical user interface (GUI).

mcqs.q: OS X uses which type of kernel?
mcqs.explanation: OS X uses a hybrid kernel — combining elements of monolithic and microkernel designs.

mcqs.q: A hybrid kernel is:
options: A combination of two different kernels
mcqs.explanation: A hybrid kernel is a combination of two different kernel types, typically blending features of monolithic and microkernel architectures.

mcqs.q: To access services of the Operating System, an interface is provided by:
mcqs.explanation: System Calls provide the interface used by user programs to access services of the Operating System.

mcqs.q: What is the main function of a command interpreter?
options: To get and execute the next user-specified command
mcqs.explanation: The main function of a command interpreter is to get and execute the next user-specified command.

mcqs.q: If a process fails, where do most operating systems write the error information?
mcqs.explanation: If a process fails, most operating systems write the error information to a log file for later analysis and debugging.

mcqs.q: DTrace is a facility that:
options: Dynamically adds probes to a running system in user process and kernel
mcqs.explanation: DTrace is a facility that dynamically adds probes to a running system, both in user processes and in the kernel — used for performance analysis and troubleshooting.

mcqs.q: Systems which allow more than one process execution at a time are called:
mcqs.explanation: Multiprogramming systems allow more than one process execution at a time, increasing CPU utilisation.

mcqs.q: What is throughput in an operating system?
options: The number of processes completed per unit time
mcqs.explanation: Throughput is the number of processes completed per unit time — a key metric of OS performance.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is an OS-level concept?
mcqs.explanation: System Calls are an OS-level concept — they form the interface between user programs and the operating system.

mcqs.q: Which OS function keeps track of the primary memory?
mcqs.explanation: Memory Management keeps track of the primary memory — what is allocated, what is free and to which process each block belongs.

mcqs.q: Which OS function is also called the I/O controller?
mcqs.explanation: Device Management is also called the I/O controller. It decides which process gets the device, when and for how long.

mcqs.q: Device Management decides:
options: Which process gets the device, when, and for how long
mcqs.explanation: Device Management (I/O controller) decides which process gets the device, when and for how long.

mcqs.q: Which OS function keeps track of time and resources used by various jobs/users?
mcqs.explanation: Job Accounting keeps track of time and resources used by various jobs and/or users.

mcqs.q: Which OS function records delays between the request for a service and the response from the system?
mcqs.explanation: Control over System Performance records delays between the request for a service and the response from the system.

mcqs.q: Which OS function helps deallocate the CPU when the processor is no longer required?
mcqs.explanation: CPU Deallocation helps deallocate the CPU when the processor is no longer required for process execution.

mcqs.q: Which OS function provides an interface to access OS services?
mcqs.explanation: System Calls provide an interface to access services of the Operating System.

mcqs.q: Which OS function decides which process gets a device, when, and for how long?
mcqs.explanation: Device Management (also called the I/O controller) decides which process gets a device, when and for how long.

mcqs.q: How many primary OS management functions are typically classified?
mcqs.explanation: There are six primary OS management functions: Memory Management, Device Management, Job Accounting, Control over System Performance, CPU Deallocation and System Calls.

mcqs.q: When a process is unable to run until some task has been completed, the process is in which state?
mcqs.explanation: A process is in the blocked state when it is unable to run until some task (e.g. I/O completion) has finished.

mcqs.q: When a process is using the CPU, it is said to be in which state?
mcqs.explanation: When a process is actively using the CPU, it is in the running state.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT contained in a Process Control Block (PCB)?
mcqs.explanation: A Process Control Block (PCB) does NOT contain a bootstrap programme. It contains process state, programme counter, registers, scheduling info and memory limits.

mcqs.q: What does PCB stand for in the context of operating systems?
mcqs.explanation: In OS context, PCB stands for Process Control Block — a data structure that stores information about each process.

mcqs.q: A process stack does NOT contain:
mcqs.explanation: A process stack does NOT contain the PID of a child process. It contains local variables, function parameters and return addresses.

mcqs.q: The address of the next instruction to be executed by the current process is provided by:
mcqs.explanation: The Programme Counter provides the address of the next instruction to be executed by the current process.

mcqs.q: A set of processes is said to be in deadlock if:
options: One process is using the CPU
Each process is blocked and will remain so forever
mcqs.explanation: A set of processes is in deadlock if each process is blocked and will remain so forever — each is waiting for a resource held by another.

mcqs.q: Which of the following best describes the running state of a process?
options: Process is in the ready queue
mcqs.explanation: In the running state, the process is actively using the CPU to execute instructions.

mcqs.q: What is the typical condition for a process to be in the blocked state?
options: It is currently using the CPU
It is unable to run until some task is completed
mcqs.explanation: A process is in the blocked state when it is unable to run until some task (typically I/O) is completed.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is true about a Process Control Block (PCB)?
options: It contains only the program counter
It is a data structure that stores process information but NOT the bootstrap programme
mcqs.explanation: A PCB is a data structure that stores process information including state, programme counter and registers — but does NOT contain a bootstrap programme.

mcqs.q: A deadlock condition is characterized by:
mcqs.explanation: A deadlock is characterised by every process in the set being blocked and remaining so forever.

mcqs.q: The programme counter is used to:
options: Hold the address of the next instruction to be executed
mcqs.explanation: The Programme Counter holds the address of the next instruction to be executed by the current process.

mcqs.q: In a Monolithic Kernel:
options: All OS services run in user space
All OS services run in kernel space
Only essential services run in kernel space
Only file system services run in kernel space
mcqs.explanation: In a Monolithic Kernel, all OS services run in kernel space. It is fast but large in size.

mcqs.q: In a Microkernel:
options: All services run in kernel space
Only essential services run in kernel space; the rest run in user space
Only device drivers run in kernel space
mcqs.explanation: In a Microkernel, only essential services run in kernel space; the rest run in user space — making it more modular.

mcqs.q: Which kernel type is more modular by design?
mcqs.explanation: A Microkernel is more modular by design because non-essential services run in user space, isolated from the core kernel.

mcqs.q: Which kernel type combines features of monolithic and microkernel?
mcqs.explanation: A Hybrid Kernel combines features of monolithic and microkernel designs. OS X uses a hybrid kernel.

mcqs.q: Which of the following operating systems uses a hybrid kernel?
mcqs.explanation: OS X uses a hybrid kernel that combines elements of monolithic and microkernel designs.

mcqs.q: How many main types of kernels are typically classified?
mcqs.explanation: Three main types of kernels are typically classified: Monolithic, Microkernel and Hybrid.

mcqs.q: The number of processes completed per unit time is called:
mcqs.explanation: Throughput is the number of processes completed per unit time.

mcqs.q: Multiprogramming refers to:
options: Running more than one process at a time
mcqs.explanation: Multiprogramming refers to systems that allow more than one process execution at a time.

mcqs.q: DTrace is primarily used for:
options: Adding probes dynamically to a running system
mcqs.explanation: DTrace is a facility that dynamically adds probes to a running system, both in user processes and the kernel — primarily used for performance analysis.

mcqs.q: The 'log file' in an operating system is used to:
options: Record error information when a process fails
mcqs.explanation: When a process fails, most operating systems write the error information to a log file.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is the correct definition of system calls?
options: Interface that provides access to OS services
mcqs.explanation: System Calls are the interface that provides user programs access to the services of the Operating System.

mcqs.q: Which of the following statements is TRUE about Linux?
options: It is the same as Windows
mcqs.explanation: Linux is an open-source operating system — its source code is freely available.

mcqs.q: Which type of interface do Unix-based systems support?
mcqs.explanation: Unix-based systems support both a command line interface (CLI) and a graphical user interface (GUI).

mcqs.q: The interface used by application programmes to request OS services is:
mcqs.explanation: Application programmes use System Calls to request services from the Operating System.

mcqs.q: Which one of the following is NOT considered an operating system?
mcqs.explanation: Oracle is NOT an operating system — it is a database management system. Linux, Windows and Mac OS are all operating systems.
